# ITR Workbook — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🔌 offline (no LLM)*

Builds a formula-driven Indian ITR input workbook from a GnuCash "Balance Sheet (eguile)" HTML export (P&L embedded via Retained Earnings). Always runs the full identity/reconciliation check suite (Imbalance == 0, Assets == Equity+Trading+Liabilities+Retained Earnings, every subtotal == sum of its children). Pick the Entity and Assessment Year from the dropdowns (populated from Data/itr/entities.yaml and Data/itr/rules/tax_rules_*.yaml); the selected AY must match the income year inferred from the HTML's own as-of date, or the run hard-fails before any schedule build is attempted. If a matching .gnucash book is also supplied, runs a book<->HTML cross-check per income/expense account. If an entity mapping is also supplied, resolves every leaf to a tag (nearest-ancestor); any unmapped account sets the run status to BLOCKED-FOR-REVIEW and writes a proposed-mappings snippet (with an LLM suggestion per account when an endpoint is configured) ready to paste into the mapping file. If a Form 16 PDF is also supplied, parses its Part B/Annexure-I salary computation, prints the employer TAN, the 115BAC(1A) opt-out election, and the form's own internal consistency checks; an encrypted PDF is decrypted automatically using the selected entity's PAN on file (no need to re-type it). When a mapping is also available, additionally cross-checks 17(1) gross salary against SALARY_GROSS and net tax payable against TAXPAID_TDS_SALARY. If a 26AS workbook is also supplied, ties out reported TDS by quarter and feeds the 234C dividend-quarter buckets. The Regime dropdown overrides the entity's configured regime for this run only (leave blank to use the entity's default for the selected AY). Marginal relief, surcharge caps, age-linked slabs/deductions, and every rate/cap/section referenced in the output are driven entirely from the Rules config for the selected year — nothing is hardcoded.

## When to use it

Use once book, mapping, and (optionally) Form 16 / 26AS inputs are ready for an entity and assessment year, to produce the standardized ITR input workbook for review or handoff to a CA. An unmapped account or a selected-AY/HTML-year mismatch blocks the run rather than silently misstating income.

## Inputs

- **Balance Sheet (eguile) HTML** (required) — accepts: HTML (.html), one file per entity.
  - The GnuCash 'Balance Sheet (eguile)' HTML export, as-of 31 March.
  - ⚠️ Must include the trailing 'Imbalance Amount' row — a truncated export (copy interrupted) is rejected with a hard failure. The as-of date must match the selected Assessment Year, or the run hard-fails before building any schedules.
- **Entity** (optional) — accepts: One of the entities defined in entities.yaml, via dropdown.
  - Which entity (from Data/itr/entities.yaml) this run is for.
  - ⚠️ Also supplies the PAN used to auto-decrypt an encrypted Form 16 — leave unset only for ad hoc/exploratory runs without a Form 16.
- **Assessment Year** (optional) — accepts: One of the years found under Data/itr/rules/, via dropdown.
  - The Assessment Year (income year) this workbook is for.
  - ⚠️ Must agree with the income year inferred from the Balance Sheet HTML's own as-of date, or the run hard-fails with a stub-only workbook.
- **Regime (blank = use the entity's configured regime)** (optional) — accepts: "new", "old", or blank to use the entity's configured regime.
  - Overrides the entity's configured tax regime for this run only.
- **GnuCash book (optional, enables CG lots + dividend quarters)** (optional) — accepts: .gnucash
- **Entity mapping (optional, enables the account-tag resolution + BLOCKED-FOR-REVIEW check)** (optional) — accepts: .yaml
- **Form 16 PDF (optional, fills the Salary sheet; password auto-derived from the selected entity's PAN if encrypted)** (optional) — accepts: .pdf, .PDF
- **26AS workbook (optional, feeds TDS tie-out + 234C quarters)** (optional) — accepts: .xlsx

## How to run

1. Pick the Balance Sheet (eguile) HTML export.
2. Select the Entity and Assessment Year from the dropdowns.
3. Optionally supply the GnuCash book, entity mapping, Form 16 PDF, and/or 26AS workbook.
4. Click Run to build the workbook and print the verification summary.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ITR.xlsx
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ITR.xlsx`** — The full ITR input workbook, or a stub with an ERROR summary if the run was blocked.

## Tips

book_file runs the book<->HTML cross-check. mapping_file runs the account-tag resolution; an unmapped account blocks the run and emits a proposed-mappings snippet next to the output file. form16_pdf runs the salary parse + internal consistency checks, plus the Book<->Form16 cross-checks when mapping_file is also supplied; the decryption password is auto-derived from the selected entity's PAN. as26_workbook ties out TDS by quarter and feeds 234C dividend-quarter bucketing. If the Entity/Assessment Year dropdowns look stale after editing entities.yaml or the rules config, use the refresh button beside them.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| "file truncated -- re-export" error. | The HTML export is missing its trailing Imbalance Amount row. Re-export from GnuCash. |
| "ERROR: selected Assessment Year does not match" and only a stub workbook is produced. | Pick the Assessment Year that matches the uploaded HTML's as-of date, or upload the HTML for the selected year, then run again. |
